title: Introduce a sentinel layer variant for zstd:chunked pulls
body:
Introduce a sentinel layer variant to safely skip the full-digest mitigation for zstd:chunked pulls.

This PR eliminates the duality by creating a separate manifest variant with a non-tar sentinel layer prepended.  Unaware clients and scanners cannot process the sentinel variant so the image no longer has two possible interpretations.

Aware clients recognize the sentinel, skip it, and pull without the expensive full-digest recomputation safely because no unaware tool can be tricked into validating the wrong content.  Data layers are reused by digest so there is no duplication.

I've pushed an image with the new format here:  quay.io/giuseppe/zstd-chunked:sentinel

@mtrmac @actionmancan this is a PoC for the idea we have discussed on how to relax the mitigation

comments.body:
FWIW, just to have it written down, I think this is a few ~separate problem spaces, probably separate PRs.
- Add a _single-image-instance_ compression/decompression implementation for the new format. It needs a name, it needs to correctly compress, decompress, re-compress from/to gzip, _perhaps_ special-case conversions from/to current zstd:chunked . Ideally this should mostly fit into `copy/compression.go` (I don’t know how we add/remove the sentinel itself)
  - The manifest updates should go through `ManifestUpdateOptions`, or maybe we should finally give up on that one (and how it uses `BlobInfo` for edits) and move to an internal-only API. The latter, if it should happen, would certainly be a separate PR.
- Add the ability to enforce chunked-only pull path for an image (disable fallbacks, or fall back to a full pull to a temporary file + serving ranges from that). That would be ~inactive at that point, but conceptually a separate chunk of effort.
- Set up pulls of sentinel images to use the optimized path
- Figure out how we are going to build the "zstd as tar"+"zstd sentinel" images that share blobs.
  - … and a variant of #201 .
